_toolchainPolicy: Tools the harness SHELLS OUT TO are pinned here by absolute path and installed to the GLOBAL/user toolchain (scoop on Windows; `uv python install` for Python). Never vendored in-project, never a foreign venv, never a bare name that PATH may resolve to a Store stub. Project LIBRARIES (pnpm/cargo/uv deps) are unaffected - this governs harness tooling only. preflight.py verifies each by EXECUTING it and fails loud.
_attributionPolicy: Claude does NOT tag itself as a git contributor by default. sync stamps settings.attribution = {commit:'', pr:''}, which removes the Co-Authored-By trailer and the PR attribution line. Set attribution.enabled=true below to restore it. (`includeCoAuthoredBy` is the deprecated spelling; `attribution` supersedes it.)
_permissionsPolicy: Profile chosen at init via MCQ; `deny` beats `ask` beats `allow`. NEVER ship Bash(*) with an empty deny list - that is root with extra steps. The deny list is the load-bearing part: destructive git (force-push, hard reset, clean -fdx, tag -d), secret reads (.env, *.pem, .ssh), and writes to generated .agents/ are blocked outright. Gate revocation (`git tag -d`) is denied to agents ON PURPOSE - it is a human-approved pivot action (principle #11).

_profiles.strict: Reads/greps free; every write and every shell command asks. Slowest, safest. Unfamiliar codebase, production repo, or you want to watch every move.
_profiles.balanced: RECOMMENDED. Reads + edits + safe git/file commands allowed; network, installers, and destructive git ask; secrets and history-rewrites denied. The harness hooks are the real enforcement (read guard, gate guard, domain boundaries).
_profiles.permissive: Most Bash allowed; only the deny list blocks. Fast, for a throwaway/sandbox repo you can delete. NOT for anything you care about.
_profiles.custom: Hand-authored allow/ask/deny in .claude/settings.json. sync will not overwrite it.

_modelPolicy: Rankings follow the token-efficiency skill's hard rule (expensive thinks / cheap types / cheapest logs) via thinker->workhorse->utility. TWO DELIBERATE DEVIATIONS, same reasoning both times - the skill's pins predate newer models at identical price: (1) the skill pins the coding-subagent orchestrator to claude-opus-4-6; we use claude-opus-4-8 - same $5/$25 per MTok, higher ceiling. (2) the skill pins coders to claude-sonnet-4-6; we use claude-sonnet-5 - same $3/$15 per MTok tier, near-Opus quality on coding and agentic work. Do not 'fix' either back. sessionDefault is the INTERACTIVE session model (Opus latest) and is deliberately NOT models.workhorse.
_terminalsPolicy: EXAMPLE - VARIABLE COUNT. This t1..t4 shape illustrates the pattern (one gate owner, one gated feature domain, one sensitive+gated domain, one never-blocked domain) - it is NOT a fixed four to fill in. Generate exactly as many tN entries as the phased plan's domains genuinely need: 1 if the domains don't split, N if they do (decision-guide.md Sec. 1's simultaneous-progress test). The two invariants that hold at ANY N: at least one terminal has neverBlocked:true, and a gate exists only when work is actively wrong without it - sync_harness.py's verify_terminal_topology() and verify_init.py both fail loud if no terminal is neverBlocked.

scaffold-complete.description: Repo scaffold done; T2/T3 may start

security-pass-domain-b.description: Security checklist passed on sensitive domain

_pivotPolicy: A pivot is HUMAN-GATED (core principle #11). No agent may unilaterally declare work dead. A pivot writes to three places: a memory/decisions/ note (narrative + pros/cons), an agent_compile_logs FAILURE row (queryable 'do not retry'), and a one-line salvage rule on the relevant agent definition. If a pivot invalidates a gate, the tag is REVOKED (git tag -d + push --delete) and must be re-earned; gate_guard.py needs no change - 'does the tag exist?' correctly answers no. See 11_pivots.md.
_gateGuardPolicy: C1/C3: the DEFAULT write sandbox for a terminal is its own git worktree (.claude/worktrees/tN/ on branch harness/tN, spawn its agents with isolation: "worktree") - a terminal cannot reach another terminal's files from inside its own checkout. gate_guard.py's job shrank to what a worktree cannot express: sensitivePaths (below) + human-gated actions, always enforced regardless of worktree. Its domain-glob check (terminals[t].paths) is kept only as a SECONDARY/advisory safety net for terminals not yet worktree-isolated, or for a Bash write that escaped its worktree - the hook's matcher never covers Bash. See 05_hooks_and_logging.md 'Worktree write-gate (C1/C3)'.
_sotPolicy: Seed categories from the project goal at init (token-efficiency references/sot-framework.md). One-offs get [SOT:IDX:name]; promote to a category at frequency >= 5 during checkpoints. Agents grep tags BEFORE reading - never blind-scan.

rtk.note: 60-90% output compression on dev CLI commands, <10ms overhead, unknown subcommands pass through. Verify with `rtk --version && rtk gain` - never reinstall blindly. DO NOT instruct agents to type the `rtk` prefix themselves: if the user-tier PreToolUse hook (~/.claude/hooks/rtk-rewrite.sh, from the token-efficiency skill) is installed, it rewrites `git status` -> `rtk git status` mechanically before execution, and already-prefixed commands pass through untouched. A prompt-level prefix instruction is redundant there and unreliable everywhere else - it decays over a session and never reaches subagents. If the hook is NOT installed, install it rather than asking the model to remember.

memory._division: vault = human-curated decisions + pivot narratives (single source of long-term truth, principle #4). shards (.agents/logs/tN.db, one per terminal, from the vendored harness/memory-schema.sql) = agent working logs, written independently per terminal. db (.agents/master.db) = DERIVED master, rebuilt from all shards by harness/build_master.py (idempotent, never hand-written) - read-only for recall. One boundary, never two sources for the same fact.
memory._compressionNote: agent_log -> agent_compile_log -> STOP. Do NOT recursively re-compress compile rows: summarizing summaries destroys the specifics a pivot exists to preserve ('LSTMs overfit on 2019 data' -> 'we tried some models'). Compile rows are indexed; recall is a WHERE clause, not a read. If it ever bloats, ARCHIVE old rows out - do not re-compress.

_skillsPolicy: A skill resolves by NAME, from the first store that has it: project .skills/<name>/SKILL.md, then user ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md, then built-in. `path` is therefore an OPTIONAL override - set it only to force a project-local copy that must NOT track the user/built-in version (pinned fork, project-specific edits). Omit it and the skill resolves wherever it actually lives. A `path` pointing at a file that does not exist is dead config: the skill still loads via its own description, so the entry never errors and the drift goes unnoticed. Filenames are SKILL.md (uppercase) - the lowercase skill.md in the original template matched nothing on a case-sensitive filesystem.

available.name: ponytail
available.loadOn: writing features, refactoring, any new abstraction
